metadata.title: Developer records desktop showing ClipXD DevTools and Konsole
metadata.description: The user runs a Lighthouse audit in Chrome DevTools showing NO_FCP errors while executing Rust cargo builds and Gemini API calls in Konsole.

visual_timeline.caption: A screenshot shows a dark interface with tabs and windows. On the left, a dark gray background displays a URL bar and browser window. On the right, a performance metrics panel shows red text labels for First Contentful Paint, Total Blocking Time, First Input Delay, Cumulative Layout Shift, and Cumulative Shift Value. The Performance metrics panel displays performance data including values for First Contentful Paint, Total Blocking Time, First Input Delay, Cumulative Layout Shift, and Cumulative Shift Value.

A screenshot shows a web page displaying performance metrics, including First Content Point, Total Blocking Time, Error, Best Content, Best SEO, Accessibility, Best Performance, and Performance score. The page indicates a performance score of 3.4 out of 5, with no errors. A warning box states there were issues affecting the load time. A URL is visible in the address bar.

A screenshot shows a web page displaying performance metrics, including first content point, total blocking time, cumulative point, error count, and cumulative point. The page shows a performance score of 0.0 out of 100, with no errors. The browser is Chrome, viewed on clipboard.com. The URL bar shows “clipboard.com”. The page includes red circular icons for performance metrics.

A screenshot shows a web page displaying performance metrics, including first content point, total blocking time, cumulative load, best content point, best load, and cumulative load. The page also shows error messages indicating issues with content and load. A browser window is visible on the left, showing a URL bar and tab bar. The right side of the screen shows additional performance metrics and error messages.

A screenshot shows a web page displaying performance metrics, including first content paint, total blocking time, cumulative layout shift, and last content paint. The page shows an error message: “There were issues affecting a line of content.” A warning box states: “The page did not paint any content. Please remove the browser window from the foreground behind the load indicator.” A link to clipboard.com is visible in the top right corner.

A screenshot shows a web page displaying performance metrics, including first content point, content point, content point, blocked loading time, total blocking time, cumulative load time, and cumulative load point. The page also shows best accessibility, best SEO, and best best practices. The URL in the browser bar is clip.com.

A screenshot shows a web page displaying performance metrics, including "Total Contentful Paint (tfm)," "Errors," "Blocking Time," "Total Blocking Time," and "Curulative Load Shift." The page also shows "Best Performance," "Accessibility," "Best Performance," "SEO," and "Lighthouse" options. A pop-up box indicates there were issues affecting the load time. A URL bar is visible at the top.

A screenshot shows a macOS system Library window. The left sidebar displays “Library,” “Recordings,” “Import,” “Settings,” and “File” options. The right sidebar shows “Library - clipboard,” “4 Clips,” “Recordings,” “Import,” “Settings,” and “File” options. The Library window displays four album art thumbnails with album titles and release dates: “4 Clips - [redacted],” “4 Clips - [redacted],” “4 Clips - [redacted],” and “4 Clips - [redacted].” Three album art thumbnails are visible in the main window: “4 Clips - [redacted],” “4 Clips - [redacted],” and “4 Clips - [redacted].” The time is 9:41 AM.

A screenshot shows a web browser displaying a library page with four video thumbnails. The top-left thumbnail shows a video titled “Screen Recording” at 8:08. The top-right thumbnail shows a video titled “Screen Recording” at 8:43. The bottom-left thumbnail shows a video titled “Screen Recording” at 8:08. The bottom-right thumbnail shows a video titled “Screen Recording” at 8:43. The browser’s address bar shows “Library - clipwd.com”. The browser’s toolbar includes tabs for “Library”, “Clip”, “Video”, “Audio”, “Import”, and “Settings”. The video thumbnails are displayed in a grid layout.

A screenshot shows a web page displaying performance metrics, including 1st Content Pointful Error (red), Total Blocking Time (red), Curtainte Length (red), Curtaint Length (red), Curtaint (red), Curtaint (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red), Curtain (red),

A terminal window displays multiple lines of code in a dark theme, predominantly black with white text. The top line reads “clip:claude=konsale,” followed by “claude=konsale” on the next line. Below are several lines of code, including “index.json in place” and “confer-opencode.” Other text includes “claude=konsale” on subsequent lines and mentions “claude=konsale” again. The terminal shows multiple tabs and windows, including “plugins,” “settings,” and “auto-update.logout.”

A terminal window displays multiple lines of code in a dark theme, primarily written in JavaScript. The top line reads “clip:claude=konsale,” and the subsequent lines show various operations including `index.json`, `confer-opencode`, `console.log`, and `console.error`. The terminal also shows standard file navigation and editing tools. The bottom line displays “Render permissions and view changes” with red and blue text indicating status updates.

A screenshot shows a web browser window displaying a text editor with lines of code. The text editor shows code snippets with comments and function definitions, including comments like “int main() { return 0; }” and “int main(void) { return 0; }”. The browser window also displays a title bar with tabs like “Home,” “File,” “Edit,” and “View,” and a URL bar showing “clipz.com”. A sidebar on the right lists “Clipz.com” and “Clipz.com: Sharing your clipboard.” A green box labeled “Clipz.com: Clipboard” is also visible.

A screenshot shows a Mac OS X Terminal window displaying a web page with a title bar reading “Recordings” and a URL “http://localhost:3000/web/recordings”. The browser’s URL bar shows “Library” and a URL starting with “http://localhost:3000/web/”. The “Recordings” tab is active, and the browser’s URL bar displays “Library” and a URL starting with “http://localhost:3000/web/”. A waveform graphic is visible below the URL bar. Multiple open tabs are visible in the browser’s tab bar, including “MCP server - connected”. The “read-ons-form-on-top-on” option is enabled in the top-right corner.

A screenshot shows a recording interface in a macOS recording application. The recording interface displays a waveform graphic, a text box showing “Recording your screen…”, and a “Stop & get link” button. The recording library shows a file path and timestamp. Various menu options are visible, including “Recordings,” “Import,” “Audio,” “Settings,” and “read-only forms on stop.”

@ Built. Now let's copy the clip (since Gemini's deep pass will overwrite in place) and run the real deep pass against the same test video for a fair comparison:
@ Now running the real Gemini deep pass against this clip, timing it:
@ The upload succeeded (auth is valid), but generateContent hit a transient 503 (Gemini overloaded) after 39s. Let me retry once — this is a documented transient error, not a real failure:
@ Two 503s in a row is suspicious — let me check if gemini-2.5-flash-lite is even a valid/current model id for this key, since my knowledge cutoff might be stale here.

© gemini-2.5-flash-Lite exists but may be getting deprioritized — the catalog now has gemini-3.1-flash-Lite (stable) as the current cheap/fast tier. Let me retry using the model override my code already supports: 6
@ Worked — 17.6 seconds total (upload + wait for ACTIVE + generate). Let's see what it actually produced: =)
© Excellent output — Gemini correctly narrated the error-then-resolved arc across the whole timeline, which no single-frame caption could represent. Let me check on the timing breakdown now.
@ That's a stdout-buffering artifact, not a real 10-minute hang — Python block-buffers non-TTY output. Let me kill it and rerun unbuffered with a smaller frame count for a fast, clean read. ba
